title: Delete dead VoiceChat.tsx component (unimported, superseded by VoiceChatTab)
description: frontend/src/components/VoiceChat.tsx (155 lines) is imported nowhere — a repo-wide grep for 'VoiceChat' shows only AgentDetail.tsx importing VoiceChatTab and ExternalVoiceChatTab, never VoiceChat. It is the old voice UI: its own statusColor/statusLabel switch (VoiceChat.tsx:5-45) duplicates STATUS_LABELS already exported by VoiceSessionContext. It is also the sole consumer of currentTranscript/currentResponse/currentFunction exposed by VoiceSessionContext (VoiceSessionContext.tsx:104-106, 924-926, 943-945), so those context fields are vestigial too.
sketch: 1) Delete frontend/src/components/VoiceChat.tsx. 2) Optionally (follow-up, context file is shared scope): remove currentTranscript/currentResponse/currentFunction state and their entries from the context value object in VoiceSessionContext.tsx if no other consumer exists (grep confirms none). Run tsc + vite build to confirm.
impact: -155 lines immediately (plus ~15 more in VoiceSessionContext); removes a misleading second voice UI that no longer reflects the real voice flow
guidance: Step 1 (delete VoiceChat.tsx) is safe as-is. Corrections for step 2: (a) the impact estimate of "~15 more lines" in VoiceSessionContext is wrong — the reviewer's case-sensitive grep missed ~45 setter call sites (setCurrentTranscript/setCurrentResponse/setCurrentFunction at VoiceSessionContext.tsx:245-898) interleaved with WebRTC/data-channel/delegation logic; full removal is a ~50+ line diff and several handler branches (e.g., the realtime-event switch at 480-521) contain only a setter call, leaving empty branches to tidy. (b) Removing those useState updates reduces provider re-render frequency and context-value identity churn for VoiceChatTab/ExternalVoiceChatTab — invisible since no consumer reads the fields, but not strictly identical render timing. Keep step 2 as a separate optional follow-up commit (as the sketch already says) or skip it; do not bundle it with the file deletion. Citation nit: VoiceChat.tsx:5-19 is statusColor, 36-45 is the statusLabel memo that duplicates STATUS_LABELS.

title: Collapse the 6 OAuth connect widgets into one OAuthConnectWidget + useConnectStatus hook
description: Six files (222+230+218+248+233+231 = 1,382 lines) are near-clones: diff(GmailConnect, GoogleDriveConnect) = 54 differing lines out of 230. Every file repeats: status state + onStatusChangeRef + pollRef (GmailConnect.tsx:14-27 = GitHubConnect.tsx:6-17 = OneDriveConnect.tsx:14-27), fetchStatus (GmailConnect.tsx:29-45), the postMessage OAuth-callback listener (GmailConnect.tsx:48-63, OneDriveConnect.tsx:48-71, GitHubConnect.tsx:37-58 — same semantics, different message type/service filter), the popup-open + popup-closed polling block (GmailConnect.tsx:65-104 = OneDriveConnect.tsx:73-112 = GitHubConnect.tsx:60-96, byte-identical except popup name), handleDisconnect, and four render states (loading / statusError+Retry / not-configured / connected card) — GmailConnect.tsx:118-229 vs OneDriveConnect.tsx:126-247. Per-provider deltas are pure data: 3 api methods, message type + optional service field, icon pair, label, button color/label, badge detail field (email/login/teamName), env-var hint text, and OneDrive's extra 'personal' consumer button.
impact: ~1,380 lines -> ~410 (shared widget+hook ~260 + 6 thin config files); adding the next OAuth provider becomes a 20-line config instead of a 230-line copy-paste; bug fixes (e.g. the popup-poll cleanup) land once instead of six times
guidance: 1) MAIN TRAP — the message listeners are NOT 'same semantics' as claimed; there are two variants. Variant A (GmailConnect.tsx:48-63, GoogleDriveConnect.tsx:41-55): guards `if ('success' in event.data)`, does NOT setConnecting(true)/setError(null) on message receipt, no try/catch. Variant B (GitHubConnect.tsx:37-58, SlackConnect.tsx:46-67, OneDriveConnect.tsx:48-71, OutlookConnect.tsx:48-70): no 'success' guard, sets connecting(true) and clears error immediately, try/catch/finally. Practical deltas: B shows the 'Connecting...' spinner during the post-callback fetchStatus while A does not; B clears a stale error on callback; A silently ignores a message lacking `success` while B would show 'OAuth failed'. Recommended unification: keep the `'success' in event.data` guard for everyone AND adopt B's setConnecting(true)/setError(null). This changes Gmail/GDrive only by a sub-second spinner + error-clear (UX-neutral or better) and changes B providers only for malformed messages that the server-rendered oauth-redirect page never sends. Do this normalization deliberately and note it in the commit message; do NOT add a per-config flag for it (over-engineering). Also note: B's try/catch around fetchStatus is dead code — fetchStatus never throws (it has its own try/catch/finally at e.g. GmailConnect.tsx:29-41) — safe to drop. 2) Config needs up to THREE icon slots, not an 'icon pair': main icon, off/error-state icon, and disconnect-button icon (Gmail: Mail/MailX/MailX; GitHub: Github/Github/Unplug; GDrive: HardDrive/HardDrive/XCircle; OneDrive: Cloud/CloudOff/CloudOff; Outlook: Mail/MailX/MailX; Slack: MessageSquare/XCircle/XCircle). Preserve these exact pairings — e.g. GitHub uses Unplug only on the disconnect button. 3) Keep popup names as per-config data verbatim — OneDrive uses 'microsoft-oauth' while Outlook uses 'outlook-oauth' despite sharing the Azure app; do not 'fix' that inconsistency. 4) extraConnectActions must be a render prop receiving { connect: (opts?) => void, connecting: boolean } so OneDrive's 'personal' button (OneDriveConnect.tsx:219-226) can call connect({ consumer: true }) and share the disabled state. 5) Type the hook's status as { configured: boolean; connected: boolean; [k: string]: any } — SlackConnect.tsx:180 reads (status as any).teamName and badge accessors differ per provider (email/login/teamName/none; OneDrive shows plain 'Connected'). 6) Preserve the two-variant connect hint (agentId vs global text, e.g. GmailConnect.tsx:222-225) via the sketched connectHint(agentId?) function, and derive the loading label / console.error prefix from config.name ('Checking Google Drive status...', 'Gmail status check failed:').

title: Collapse the 3 credential-form connect widgets (Jira, S3, WordPress) into one CredentialConnectWidget
description: Same clone family as the OAuth widgets but with an inline credentials form instead of a popup: JiraConnect.tsx:13-110, S3Connect.tsx:5-104 and WordPressConnect.tsx:13-110 are line-for-line identical (state, fetchStatus, handleConnect validate-then-api-then-clear, handleDisconnect), and the loading/statusError/connected-card renders repeat again (JiraConnect.tsx:79-152 = WordPressConnect.tsx:79-152). Only the field definitions differ: Jira domain/email/apiToken (JiraConnect.tsx:154-188), S3 accessKeyId/secretAccessKey/region/endpoint (S3Connect.tsx:148-195), WordPress siteUrl/username/applicationPassword (WordPressConnect.tsx:154-188) — each field is just {label, placeholder, type, required, helpText}.
impact: ~650 lines -> ~280 (widget ~140 + 3 configs); together with the OAuth proposal the 9 connect widgets shrink from ~2,030 to ~700 lines
guidance: Sketch is sound but incomplete on five behavior-preservation traps: (1) Tailwind JIT: do NOT interpolate `bg-${color}-500/5` etc. — classes must appear literally in source or they get purged. Implement color as a lookup table mapping 'blue'|'orange'|'sky' to full literal class strings for each slot (card bg/border, icon, badge, connect button, focus:border-*). (2) S3 validation differs in two ways the sketch glosses over: only accessKeyId+secretAccessKey are required (region/endpoint are not, despite region being sent), and the validation error message differs — S3Connect.tsx:41 says 'Access Key ID and Secret Access Key are required' while Jira/WP say 'All fields are required'. Add a `requiredError?: string` (or derive message from required field labels) and drive both the pre-submit check and the submit button's disabled expression from the same required-field set. (3) S3 region initializes to 'us-east-1' and resets to 'us-east-1' (not '') after successful connect (S3Connect.tsx:15,51) — reset each field to its `initial ?? ''`, not blanket ''. (4) S3's IAM-console help paragraph (S3Connect.tsx:190-195) is a sibling of the field divs at form level, unlike Jira/WP whose help nests under a field; attaching it as the endpoint field's `help` changes DOM placement and spacing slightly — add an optional `formFooter?: ReactNode` to the config to preserve layout exactly. (5) WordPress badge detail is `status.siteUrl ? new URL(status.siteUrl).host : 'Connected'` (WordPressConnect.tsx:125) — new URL() can throw on malformed input; keep that exact expression inside the WP badgeDetail closure (don't wrap in try/catch — preserve existing behavior). Also: keep per-provider connect closures calling the existing api functions verbatim — api.connectS3 has the surprising arg order (agentId, secretAccessKey, accessKeyId, region, boardId?, endpoint?) and the widget passes `endpoint || undefined` (S3Connect.tsx:47, api.ts:941); preserve the `agentId || ''` / `boardId || undefined` coercions. Field `type` must be configurable per field (email for Jira email, password for all secrets, text otherwise). Finally, the sketch depends on useConnectStatus from the separate OAuth proposal — if that one isn't done first, inline the hook (it's only the fetchStatus/loading/statusError/onStatusChangeRef block) rather than coupling the two changes.

title: Split the 1,437-line AdminPanel into one file per tab
description: AdminPanel.tsx is a god component: ~30 useState hooks for 4 independent tabs are interleaved at the top (users 34-39, settings 42-56, reset 53-56, llm 58-62, voice-test 64-87, boards 89-97), loaders at 99-197, handlers at 199-398, then four giant conditional render blocks: Settings 473-907, Users 910-1150, LLM 1153-1236, Boards 1239-1431. No state is shared across tabs except showToast (the Settings tab re-loads llmConfigs itself at 196-197, proving independence). Bonus smells: the 4 tab buttons at 423-466 are copy-pasted markup differing only in icon/label (ProjectDetailModal.tsx:10-17 already shows the TABS-array pattern), and ToggleLeft/ToggleRight/UserCheck are imported (line 4) but never used.
sketch:
New frontend/src/components/admin/ directory:
  UsersTab.tsx({ showToast, onImpersonate, onClose })   // users state + handleCreate/Update/Delete/Impersonate + ROLE_CONFIG + timeAgo
  SettingsTab.tsx({ showToast })                          // settings, reminderConfig, resetRoles, voiceTest, loads its own llmConfigs
  LlmConfigsTab.tsx({ showToast })                        // llmConfigs + LlmConfigModal wiring
  BoardsTab.tsx({ showToast })                            // boardsList + counts + board form/columns editor
AdminPanel.tsx keeps only the shell (~120 lines): overlay, header, a TABS = [{id,label,icon}] array mapped to buttons, and {activeTab === id && <Tab .../>}. Pure cut-and-paste moves — each tab's state/handlers/JSX are already disjoint blocks.
impact: 1,437-line file -> ~120-line shell + 4 tabs of 150-450 lines each; each tab's state lives next to its JSX instead of 800 lines above it; ~40 lines of duplicated tab-button markup and dead imports removed
guidance: Behavior-preservation traps the sketch glosses over: (1) Today all tab state lives in the always-mounted AdminPanel, so switching tabs preserves it; the sketched {activeTab === id && <Tab/>} unmounts tabs and resets their state. Concretely lost on a tab round-trip: half-filled create-user form (showCreateForm/createForm/editingUser, lines 36-39), an open board edit form (boardEditingId/boardCreating/boardForm, lines 94-96 — loadBoards re-runs on activation today but does NOT reset the form), and voice-test result banners (voiceTest, line 65). Settings/reminder edits are ALREADY discarded on return (line 149 re-fetches), so those are unaffected. To preserve behavior exactly: keep all four tab components mounted inside the shell and toggle visibility with CSS, passing an `active` prop whose effect drives the activation loads (reproducing lines 149/181/195/197); otherwise accept the state-reset as a deliberate minor UX change, not an invisible one. (2) loadUsers (line 111) runs once on AdminPanel mount and never on tab switches; naive conditional mounting adds a users re-fetch on every return to the Users tab — preserved exactly by the always-mounted variant. (3) Correction to the description: llmConfigs IS shared between Settings (lines 627, 656) and the LLM tab; the sketch's per-tab loadLlmConfigs is fine because line 197 already re-fetches on activation, but duplicate the ~12-line loader (or a tiny useLlmConfigs hook) — do not try to keep it in the shell. (4) Keep AdminPanel.tsx at frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx as the default-exported shell so Dashboard.tsx:24's lazy(() => import('./AdminPanel')) and its chunk boundary are untouched. (5) timeAgo (line 10) and ROLE_CONFIG (line 26) move into UsersTab.tsx; do NOT merge timeAgo with the different timeAgo in frontend/src/components/tasks/taskConstants.ts:55 (different signature/output). (6) UsersTab needs { showToast, onImpersonate, onClose } — handleImpersonate (line 379) calls both onImpersonate and onClose.

title: ExternalVoiceChatTab reimplements lib/externalVoiceClient.ts inline (worklet, VAD, STT/TTS sockets duplicated)
description: lib/externalVoiceClient.ts opens with 'Reusable browser-side STT + TTS pipeline shared by the text chat and the external-voice tab' (lines 1-3) — but the external-voice tab does NOT use it; only agentDetail/ChatTab.tsx does. ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx carries its own byte-identical copies: WORKLET_CODE (ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx:38-76 = externalVoiceClient.ts:22-59), the four VAD constants (31-34 = 15-18), the STT socket open/message/VAD-finalize logic (openStt 259-313 + onWorkletFrame 316-337 vs SttSession.start 89-161 + onFrame 218-239), and PCM playback (enqueuePcmChunk 160-183 vs TtsPlayer). The stale comment actively misleads: a fix to the lib's VAD will silently not apply to the tab.
sketch:
Two options, pick by appetite:
A (full): extend SttSession with a 'keepMicAcrossUtterances' mode + reconnect-retry (the tab's only real behavioral additions, ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx:297-312 and 339-380), then rewrite the tab to drive SttSession/TtsPlayer via callbacks — tab drops to ~250 lines of pure UI + turn loop.
B (minimal, near-zero risk): export WORKLET_CODE, SILENCE_MS/MIN_SPEECH_MS/RMS_SPEECH/RMS_SILENCE, and a shared createPcmWorkletNode(ctx, targetRate) + decodePcm16ToBuffer helper from externalVoiceClient.ts and import them in the tab; fix the lib header comment to state who actually uses what.
Either way the contradiction between comment and reality disappears.
impact: Option A: -≈220 lines and one source of truth for the audio pipeline; option B: -≈80 lines but eliminates the dangerous double-maintained worklet/VAD constants
guidance: Do Option B only; treat Option A as out of scope for a behavior-preserving pass. Concretely: export WORKLET_CODE, SILENCE_MS/MIN_SPEECH_MS/RMS_SPEECH/RMS_SILENCE, and a decodePcm16ToBuffer(ctx, arrayBuf, sampleRate) helper from frontend/src/lib/externalVoiceClient.ts; import them in frontend/src/components/ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx; rewrite the lib header comment to state that SttSession/TtsPlayer are used only by agentDetail/ChatTab.tsx while the external-voice tab shares only the low-level audio primitives. When sharing WORKLET_CODE, keep the lib's version (the tab's two extra inline comments are the only difference). Traps if anyone attempts Option A later: (1) tab's onWorkletFrame checks mutedRef before sending PCM AND before VAD — mute freezes VAD state; (2) enqueuePcmChunk drops chunks when speakerOff at both enqueue and play time; (3) tab treats STT 'error' messages as non-fatal (keeps listening), SttSession tears down; (4) SttSession arms a 10s finalize timeout the tab lacks; (5) TtsPlayer creates/closes an AudioContext per reply, the tab reuses one per session; (6) status timing differs (lib fires 'listening' before getUserMedia, tab only on WS open). No frontend test coverage exists for these paths — Option A requires manual verification with live STT/TTS services.

title: Dashboard: remove phantom 'processes'/'about' views and unify the duplicated valid-view lists
description: Dashboard.tsx:38 accepts hash views ['agents','tasks','projects','budget','processes','about'], and the hashchange handler at line 104 accepts ['agents','tasks','projects','budget','about'] — two out-of-sync inline lists. But the render switch (lines 394-573) only handles tasks/projects/budget/agents: navigating to #about or #processes shows a header over a blank main area. Nothing in frontend/src links to either hash (repo grep). These are leftovers from removed views.
sketch: const VALID_VIEWS = ['agents', 'tasks', 'projects', 'budget'] as const; use it in both the useState initializer (line 38) and the hashchange effect (line 104). Delete 'processes' and 'about'.
impact: Removes 2 dead view ids and a silent blank-screen path; the two lists can never drift again
guidance: One implementation trap in the sketch: `as const` will fail the frontend tsc gate. With `const VALID_VIEWS = ['agents','tasks','projects','budget'] as const`, the call `VALID_VIEWS.includes(hash)` is a type error because `hash` is `string` and a readonly literal tuple's `.includes` expects the literal union. Either declare it without `as const` (inferred `string[]`), annotate `const VALID_VIEWS: string[] = [...]`, or cast at the call sites `(VALID_VIEWS as readonly string[]).includes(hash)`. Also hoist the constant to module scope (outside the component) so the hashchange effect at Dashboard.tsx:101-108 can reference it without adding a dependency to its empty deps array. Behavior note to accept knowingly: hand-typed `#about`/`#processes` URLs currently yield a blank main area; after the change they fall back to 'tasks' (on load) or are ignored (on hashchange) — intended, since nothing in the repo emits those hashes.

title: ProjectStats: 'embedded' prop is always true — delete the standalone-modal code path
description: ProjectStats has exactly one call site: ProjectDetailModal.tsx:411 renders <ProjectStats projectName={...} onClose={() => {}} embedded />. The non-embedded branches are therefore dead: the wrapper-class ternary (ProjectStats.tsx:181), the standalone header with title (184-189), and the close button calling the no-op onClose (204-208). The onClose={() => {}} stub at the call site is the tell that the API has rotted.
sketch: Change signature to ProjectStats({ projectName, days? }) — drop onClose and embedded. Keep only the embedded rendering ('space-y-5' wrapper, no title header, no close button). Update ProjectDetailModal.tsx:411 to <ProjectStats projectName={project.name} />.
impact: -≈25 lines; component API matches reality instead of advertising a modal mode that no longer exists
guidance: Three corrections to the sketch: (1) ProjectStats.tsx:190 — the controls row is `flex items-center gap-2 ${embedded ? 'ml-auto' : ''}` inside the justify-between flex at line 183; when removing the conditional, keep `ml-auto` unconditionally (it right-aligns the days select + refresh button once the header is gone; dropping it breaks layout). (2) Remove the now-unused `BarChart3` and `X` imports from line 9 or tsc/lint will flag them. (3) Do NOT add the sketched optional `days` prop — `days` is internal state (line 73) driven by the in-component select and no caller passes it; new signature should be just `ProjectStats({ projectName })`. Also leave the loading-state wrapper at line 95 (`bg-dark-800 border border-purple-500/30 rounded-xl p-6`) untouched — it renders that styling unconditionally today and is not part of the embedded/standalone split.

title: AddAgentModal: dead 'agents' prop and 4x repeated 'gpt-realtime' model sniffing
description: AddAgentModal.tsx:5 destructures agents = [] but the identifier never appears again in the 570-line body — Dashboard.tsx:583 passes agents={agents} for nothing. Separately, the magic substring check c.model.includes('gpt-realtime') is copy-pasted 4 times (lines 335, 360, 495, 506) with slightly different surrounding logic (auto-pick on voice toggle, auto-pick on pipeline switch, option filtering, warning banner) — easy for the literal to drift if the realtime model id changes.
impact: Dead prop removed from a public-looking component API; one definition of 'what is a realtime LLM config' instead of four string literals
guidance: Corrections: the duplication count is 5 in AddAgentModal.tsx, not 4 — also convert line 365 (sel?.model?.includes('gpt-realtime') in the voiceMode='external' branch) using isRealtimeLlm(sel); it is the same predicate applied to the selected config rather than a list search. Prefer placing isRealtimeLlm in a small shared module (e.g. frontend/src/utils) rather than file-local, because SettingsTab.tsx:258 and :279 contain the identical copy-pasted predicate and should use it too. The findRealtimeLlm wrapper adds nothing over llmConfigs.find(isRealtimeLlm); skip it or keep it — either is fine. Traps: do NOT touch the user-facing string "gpt-realtime-1.5" in the warning copy (AddAgentModal.tsx:507, SettingsTab.tsx:280) — that is display text, not the predicate; and keep the helper returning a boolean via !!/optional chaining so undefined-model configs stay excluded exactly as today.

title: Dashboard: one NAV constant for mobile menu and desktop view-switcher
description: The same four views are declared twice: the mobile menu maps over an inline array [{key:'agents',label:'Agents',icon:Users},...] (Dashboard.tsx:210-215), while the desktop switcher hand-writes four near-identical <button> blocks (252-291) differing only in view key, icon, label and title. Adding a view today requires editing three places (plus the VALID_VIEWS lists from the other proposal).
sketch:
const NAV_VIEWS = [
  { key: 'agents', label: 'Agents', icon: Users, title: 'Agents view' },
  { key: 'tasks', label: 'Workflows', icon: KanbanSquare, title: 'Workflows board' },
  { key: 'projects', label: 'Projects', icon: Tag, title: 'Projects' },
  { key: 'budget', label: 'Budget', icon: DollarSign, title: 'Budget' },
];
Map it in both the mobile dropdown and the desktop switcher (the active/inactive classNames already match per group).
impact: -≈35 lines; nav items defined once; pairs naturally with the VALID_VIEWS cleanup
guidance: Share only the DATA, keep two separate render maps — the groups intentionally differ and must not be unified: (1) mobile buttons have NO title attribute; do not spread NAV_VIEWS.title onto them. (2) Mobile classes: gap-3 w-full px-4 py-2.5, inactive 'text-dark-300 hover:bg-dark-700/50 hover:text-dark-100'; desktop classes: gap-1.5 px-3 py-2, inactive 'text-dark-400 hover:text-dark-200'. (3) Mobile onClick also calls setMobileMenuOpen(false); desktop onClick is bare setActiveView. (4) Desktop label is wrapped in <span className="hidden md:inline">; mobile label is bare text. (5) For the paired VALID_VIEWS idea: do NOT derive the hash whitelists at Dashboard.tsx:38 and :104 from NAV_VIEWS — they include 'about' (and :38 also 'processes'), which are valid hash views but not nav buttons, and the two lists differ from each other.

title: Three private timeAgo/formatDate implementations -> one lib/time.ts (+ fold GitHubActivityModal's duplicated fetch effect)
description: Relative-time formatting exists three times with subtly different outputs: AdminPanel.tsx:10-24 (timeAgo: 'just now/5m ago/3h ago/2d ago/1mo ago'), GitHubActivityModal.tsx:65-76 (formatDate: 'just now/5h ago/3d ago/locale date'), tasks/taskConstants.ts:55-64 (timeAgo). Users see inconsistent labels for the same age depending on the screen. Same file, second smell: GitHubActivityModal defines loadActivity (45-52) and then a mount useEffect (54-63) that re-implements the identical fetch inline instead of calling it — only the cancelled flag differs.
sketch: New frontend/src/lib/time.ts: export function timeAgo(date: string | Date | null): string (single bucket table: <60s 'just now', <60m '{m}m ago', <24h '{h}h ago', <30d '{d}d ago', else locale date). Replace the three locals; delete AdminPanel.tsx:10-24 and GitHubActivityModal.tsx:65-76, re-export from taskConstants.ts for its existing importers if desired. In GitHubActivityModal, change the mount effect to `useEffect(() => { loadActivity(); }, [loadActivity]);` (loadActivity already sets loading/error and is keyed by owner/repo/boardId; the cancelled-flag protection can move inside it or be accepted as-is since setData on an unmounted modal is a no-op in React 18).
impact: -≈30 lines, consistent relative-time wording across Users list, GitHub activity and task cards; one fetch path in GitHubActivityModal
guidance: 1) Not literally behavior-preserving — get owner sign-off on three visible wording changes as intentional: AdminPanel users older than 30d ('2mo ago' -> locale date, or keep a months bucket), GitHub commits under 1h ('just now' -> 'Xm ago'), task screens older than 30d ('Xd ago' -> locale date; taskConstants currently has an unbounded days tail). 2) MUST preserve 'never' at AdminPanel.tsx:1076 (user.last_seen null): either timeAgo(date, { empty: 'never' }) or keep the fallback at the call site; the unified helper's default for falsy input must stay '' because taskConstants callers render it inline (TaskCard.tsx:407). 3) Do NOT touch taskConstants.ts:66-72 formatDate — it is a DIFFERENT function (absolute locale date-time, returns null) used for tooltips and a truthiness check at HistoryDetailModal.tsx:328-329; it shares a name with GitHubActivityModal's local relative formatDate. Replace the modal's local with the new timeAgo; leave taskConstants.formatDate alone. Re-exporting timeAgo from taskConstants keeps its 5 import sites untouched. 4) In the effect fold, keep stale-response/unmount protection by moving a request-id ref or cancelled check inside loadActivity rather than dropping it (3 lines, removes the only behavioral delta); the sketch's `useEffect(() => { loadActivity(); }, [loadActivity])` form is correct — do not return the promise from the effect. 5) Verified no test files reference timeAgo/formatDate/'just now', so no test expectations break; frontend gate is tsc+vite only.

title: Extract useClickOutside / useEscapeKey hooks (7+ hand-rolled copies)
description: The mousedown-outside-ref pattern is hand-rolled in Dashboard.tsx twice back-to-back (110-119 for mobileMenuRef, 121-130 for userMenuRef — identical bodies), plus ShareBoardModal.tsx:51-57, AllCommitsDiffModal.tsx:281-298, and again in tasks/BoardTabs.tsx, tasks/KanbanColumn.tsx, tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx. The Escape-to-close effect is likewise copied in GitHubActivityModal.tsx:34-38, ProjectDetailModal.tsx:39-43, ShareBoardModal.tsx:59-63 (and tasks/CreateTaskModal, tasks/HistoryDetailModal). GitHubActivityModal.tsx:40-43 and ProjectDetailModal.tsx:45-48 also both copy the body-overflow lock.
impact: -≈60 lines across 7+ files; dismiss behavior (capture phase, cleanup) defined once
guidance: One factual error in the proposal: the impact line claims dismiss behavior uses "capture phase" — it does not. All 7 mousedown copies and all 7 keydown copies register plain bubble-phase listeners (no third argument). The hooks MUST register bubble-phase listeners too; adding { capture: true } as a "best practice" would change behavior (e.g., dismiss would fire even when an inner element stopPropagation()s in bubble phase). Behavior-preservation traps for the implementer: (1) Keep the null-ref guard semantics exactly: when ref.current is null, do nothing. Writing `if (!ref.current?.contains(e.target as Node)) onOutside()` inverts this and fires onOutside while the ref is unattached. (2) Callback freshness: AllCommitsDiffModal's Escape handler closes over showConfirm/revertMode (effect deps line 298) and TaskDetailModal's over editing/task.text (line 99). Either make the hook effect depend on the callback identity (callers pass inline arrows, so it re-registers per render — fine), or store the callback in a ref updated every render with [] effect deps. Do NOT have callers wrap handlers in useCallback with narrowed deps. (3) Enabled gating must early-return before addEventListener (Dashboard 111/122, BoardTabs 24, KanbanColumn 24 attach no listener when closed); BoardTabs passes enabled = !!contextMenu (contextMenu is an object, not boolean). (4) useBodyScrollLock cleanup must restore overflow to '' exactly as both copies do (GitHubActivityModal.tsx:42, ProjectDetailModal.tsx:47) — do not "improve" it to save/restore the previous value. (5) tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx:80-87 is one handler checking two refs (statusRef, refineRef); migrate it as two useClickOutside calls — equivalent since the two state updates are independent. (6) Keep 'mousedown' (not 'click') as the event type. Scope note: the proposal's files list omits the tasks/* components it cites in the description; either include them in the migration or state explicitly that they are a follow-up so the duplication is not half-removed.
